# AI Usage Monitor v2.1.0

This feature release makes quota-window handling adapt to what each provider
actually reports instead of assuming that every account always has both 5-hour
and 7-day limits.

## Changes

- Normalize Claude Code, Codex, and Antigravity quota responses into a shared
  dynamic-window model while keeping provider-specific API parsing.
- Read Codex `limit_window_seconds`, order windows by their real duration, and
  correctly handle accounts that currently report a weekly window only.
- Stop turning missing API windows into misleading `0%` rows.
- Render the taskbar component as one content-sized, single-line status badge
  per provider. During normal operation the badge pins the 5-hour window, or
  the shortest available window when 5h is absent; another window takes over
  only after reaching 90%, when the badge turns red and adds its countdown.
- Derive compact labels such as `5h`, `1d`, `7d`, `30d`, and `365d`
  from reported durations, with a localized quota fallback for unknown
  windows.
- Show every reported window in the detail popup, while the separate floating
  monitor keeps at most the two highest-usage windows per provider with aligned
  labels, percentages, countdowns, and micro gauges.
- Refresh the open detail popup's elapsed and next-poll countdowns every second
  without increasing provider polling, and make the popup movable by default
  with a temporary position lock without persisting the moved position.
- Migrate existing v2.0 usage caches without recreating the old missing-window
  placeholder, preserve per-provider freshness timestamps, and discard only
  provider sections that are actually stale.
- Generalize reset notifications from fixed 5h/7d wording to short- and
  long-window resets.
- Handle HTTP 429 and `Retry-After` consistently across Claude Code, Codex,
  and Antigravity, including both delay-seconds and HTTP-date values.
- Coalesce overlapping automatic and manual refresh requests into one poll,
  and discard results superseded by a newer request.
- Resume authentication polling when credentials change for any enabled
  provider, including same-length credential-file replacements.
- Keep the widget provider order synchronized with unambiguous taskbar tray
  icon reordering while preserving the configured order as a safe fallback;
  confirm drag changes after 120ms and update the taskbar and floating surfaces
  together before persisting the new order.
- Move polling timers to a process-level hidden controller so provider polling
  continues at the configured cadence while Windows is locked, RDP is
  disconnected, or Explorer is rebuilding the taskbar.
- Recover tray icons and taskbar embedding immediately from cached state on
  session, display, and `TaskbarCreated` events without issuing a wake-time
  provider request.
- Validate the widget's live taskbar parent and child-window style before
  recovery, so a transiently incomplete multi-monitor taskbar enumeration
  cannot detach a still-healthy widget or strand tray interactions.
- Keep the taskbar widget hidden when verified embedding is unavailable instead
  of exposing it as a desktop fallback popup.
- Add explicit taskbar-widget position reset and an independent compact
  floating copy of the widget with whole-surface dragging, remembered
  position, click-through to details, primary-work-area reset controls, and an
  8-logical-pixel work-area safety margin. Keep it above normal windows and
  remove the taskbar-only divider; its position is always draggable rather
  than governed by a persistent floating-window lock.
- Keep compact taskbar and floating duration labels and countdowns
  language-neutral with fixed English units (`d`, `h`, `m`, `s`, and `now`)
  while leaving the detail popup fully localized.
- Consolidate immediate and scheduled refresh controls into one Refresh
  submenu, with Now at the top followed by the polling intervals.
- Read supported Codex Desktop/CLI `auth.json` sessions and the official direct
  Windows keyring mapping without invoking the Codex executable.
- Use Windows system colours and background-aware text roles in High Contrast
  mode, keeping warning rows legible on the canvas and all warning/error pill
  foregrounds readable on the system highlight fill.
- Separate stale/error semantics from quota warnings: error pills stay neutral
  except for the red `!`, so a cached percentage is not mistaken for dangerous
  usage.
- Use official provider marks in exact-DPI tiles throughout the detail popup,
  taskbar badges, floating monitor, and custom taskbar hover cards.
- Format tray hover text as a short title followed by one quota window per
  line, with full reset timing on provider icons and a compact provider summary
  on the single app icon.
- Give each provider tray icon a stable GUID, negotiate the modern Shell
  callback contract, support keyboard activation, and restore notification-area
  focus after context menus.
- Add a top-level Icons toggle that switches between the three live provider
  icons and one neutral app icon; replace the inherited artwork with an
  independent DPI-aware multi-provider icon whose horizontal usage bars mirror
  the compact surfaces and are shared by the executable.
- Require the exact release executable name, cap update downloads, and verify a
  re-downloaded draft release plus GitHub build provenance before publishing.
- Scale the taskbar widget, detail popup, and floating window from each
  window's own DPI, and apply Windows' suggested rectangle when a top-level
  window receives `WM_DPICHANGED`.
- Report settings and usage-cache read, parse, serialization, temporary-write,
  replacement, and cleanup failures in the diagnostic log; keep settings disk
  I/O outside the shared application-state lock.
- Pin the Rust release toolchain and enforce strict Clippy checks in CI.

## Upgrade note

The stable tray-icon identity may reset the three notification icons' saved
positions once when upgrading to v2.1.0. Subsequent upgrades retain that
identity; drag the icons into the preferred order again if needed.
